entry.title: Re: yaai - ABAP AI tools - Evolving Toward Agentic AI
entry.content:
Hi @peter_walcz79 ,

I think exposing a yaai agent as a tool for an MCP is actually quite simple. You could just expose it through an ICF service, like a REST endpoint. The tricky part is making the agent behave like a tool. That can get complex depending on the tasks you expect the agent to perform.

I've recently been working on an autonomous agent feature. The idea is to define a task flow for the agent to execute.

We assign tools that allow the agent to retrieve the tasks it needs to perform and update their status during execution.

For example, I’ve been testing this feature with an agent that runs an ATC check, analyzes the results, and sends them by email.

This autonomous agent could be triggered by an ICF service handler with just a single method call.

Example:

https://github.com/christianjianelli/yaai/blob/main/src/yr_aai_agent_run.prog.abap

REPORT yr_aai_agent_run.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
PARAMETERS p_agent TYPE c LENGTH 32 OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS p_api   TYPE yde_aai_api OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS p_new   AS CHECKBOX.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  ycl_aai_agent=>run_autonomously(
    EXPORTING
      i_agent_id = CONV #( p_agent )
      i_api      = p_api
      i_new_run  = p_new
  ).

Thank you for your response!
I’d like to share a bit more background about our organization:
We are a multinational company where managers are responsible for several departments, and different business areas report to various senior leaders.
For instance, for Manager C:
Business 1 reports to Unit 1, so the approval workflow is A-B-C (primary role)-D-E.
Business 2 reports to Unit 2, so the workflow is F-G-C (concurrent role 1)-E.
Business 3 reports to Unit 3, with a workflow of H-I-C (concurrent role 2)-J-K.

As a result, when handling approval workflows and Comp. Forms, it’s necessary to switch between different roles.
However, since all three roles are within the same department, it can be challenging to quickly select the correct role for approval.
For this reason, I would like to look into other possible solutions.

Thanks,

JingYuan

entry.title: Equipment User Status Functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

The client has a requirement to maintain Equipment User Status in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The status should be updated automatically based on Maintenance Request processing through additional business logic/custom logic.

However, we could not find standard functionality for Equipment User Status management in the Public Cloud system.

Current requirement examples:

  • If equipment is under breakdown maintenance request → automatically update status as “Breakdown”
  • If equipment is idle/running with zero KM/hours → automatically update status as “Idle”
  • Status should be reflected at Equipment master level automatically based on business conditions

We would like guidance on the following:

  1. Is Equipment User Status functionality available or planned for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Is there any standard alternative recommended by SAP?
  3. Can this requirement be achieved using Custom Logic (BAdI), Custom Business Objects, Characteristics/Classifications, or side-by-side extensibility?
  4. Are there any released APIs/CDS Views/BAdIs available for updating custom equipment statuses automatically?

As a temporary workaround, we are considering using Classification Characteristics as a custom status field, but this has limitations for mass processing and standard status management.

Kindly advise the best recommended approach for Public Cloud.

Thanks & Regards

Mohit Mishra

@SAP @SAPPM @SAPEAMPS

entry.title: Re: Arrears By Net Due Date different between FBL5H and F...
entry.content:
@Megha03_01-07 

In cases where the payment term is blank and the net due date is identical in both FBL5N and FBL5H, the difference arises from how each report interprets arrears:

  • FBL5N (Customer Line Item Display): This report calculates arrears strictly at the item level. If the payment term is blank, the system does not derive any overdue calculation, so the “Arrears by Net Due Date” field remains 0.

  • FBL5H (Customer Balance Display): This report applies aging logic. When payment terms are blank, it defaults to comparing the net due date against the current system date. As a result, the arrears value is shown as current date – net due date, even though FBL5N shows zero.

This difference is expected behavior because FBL5H is designed to provide an aging overview, while FBL5N is focused on transactional detail.

entry.title: Re: Cost center is not coming in document header level wh...
entry.content: The issue seems to be related to how RE-FX transfers account assignment data into FI during RERAPP postings. For contracts not linked to rental objects, the system does not automatically derive the Cost Center unless specific configuration is in place. While the Cost Center is visible in the contract account assignment, it is not being passed to FI line items. This typically requires either: (a) defining derivation rules in RE-FX posting parameters, (b) setting up FI substitution logic, or (c) implementing a BAdI to ensure the Cost Center flows from contract to FI. I recommend reviewing the posting configuration for the new contract type and check whether substitutions or BAdI enhancements are needed to achieve dynamic Cost Center transfer.

entry.title: Re: How to derive the Sold-to Party on COGS Split posting...
entry.content:
Why it happens is because, during goods issue (COIE) in intercompany flows, the system derives the customer from the billing/SD document. For the COGS split accounts (971xxx), the derivation often picks up the intercompany customer (S0408) because that is the partner in the delivery from France (0055) to UK (0408). The final sold-to party (VBAK-KUNNR) is only available in the sales order of the selling company (0408 UK), but the COGS posting originates in the delivering company (0055 France). Hence, the wrong customer flows into CO‑PA, distorting margin reporting.

You can explore the below Standard options

1) Derivation Rules in CO‑PA (KE4U / Fiori equivalent):
You can set up a derivation rule to replace the customer in COGS postings with the sold-to party from the sales order. This requires ensuring the sales order reference is available in the accounting document (check field mapping in ACDOCA).

2) BAdI Enhancements:
SAP provides BAdIs such as BADI_SD_ACCOUNTING or BADI_AC_DOCUMENT where you can override account assignment logic. You can fetch VBAK-KUNNR from the sales order and substitute it into the CO‑PA line for COGS accounts.

3) Substitution in FI (GGB1):
Define a substitution rule for customer field in FI documents when account = 971xxx. Pull sold-to party from sales order reference. This is simpler but less flexible than a BAdI.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition generated for non-finalized Sale...
entry.content:
Dear @Kriszti,

How is the Finalisation recorded in SAP ?
We can any use of the below method:
1. Using Delivery Block in sales order
2. Using Flexible Workflow for sales order


1. Delivery Block:
Use the Delivery Block functionality, with Purchase requistion block which prevents the creation of PR when the sales order is blocked.
PR is created only when the delivery block is removed.

Assign the custom delivery block to the sales document type and this block is to be removed only after the finalisation of the sales order.

2. Flexible workflow:
Use the Flexible workflow in sales documents to block the sales orders created in this document type.
Select the "create purchase requistion" option in "Assign reason for Approval request configuration step as shown below.
This will prevent the creation of the PR in background.

Note: when the sales order is in approval process, it does not allow the users to edit the sales order directly, users have to withdraw the sales order from approval or approver should click the "rework" option to do the quantity changes.



Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: [SAC Planning] Is it realistic to operate a “Category...
entry.content:
@79 

Performance is subjective and also depends on tenant sizing. 5000 to 50000 members can run fine if the tenant has sufficient memory and CPU allocations. Significant volume growth with inadequate tenant sizing can choke the overall performance.

You should think about the below considerations.

  • How many of the 50000 members will be actually booked.
  • Will the data volume grow while moving from # category to new category keys. Is the record splitting from 1:N
  • You will also have to think about rolling the members of this new category dimension into appropriate hierarchy for better scoping in stories and data action.
  • If rows displayed in stories are only 100, then is this the aggregated view or most category members are unbooked.

If its all about data analysis by a new category dimension which is concatenation of 3 existing attributes, you could try a calculated dimension in the story using the 3 existing attributes and it will provide you the category dimension for analysis.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Norway: Auto population of Payment Reference in Billi...
entry.content:
Note this response is from SAP  Joule- J4C

 

Auto-Populating Payment Reference Field (KIDNO) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Current State Analysis

The standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality has significant limitations for auto-generating payment reference numbers (KIDNO) in outgoing invoices. The Payment Reference field (BSEG-KIDNO) is a standard system field that is not exposed for substitution or extensibility through available frameworks like BAdI FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION or Manage Substitution and Validation Rules [1]. This creates a critical gap for Norwegian business requirements where KID numbers (kundeidentifikasjon) are essential for electronic payment processing and bank statement reconciliation.

Available Solutions

Option 1: Contract Accounting Module Approach

For organizations using Contract Accounting, SAP provides built-in payment reference generation capabilities:

  • Configure payment reference models through the "Maintain Payment Reference Activation" activity (ID: 106948) [2]
  • Activate payment reference generation by assigning your company code to the Contract Accounting application
  • This automatically generates payment references when posting outgoing invoices in the Contract Accounting module
Option 2: Convergent Invoicing for Nordic Countries

SAP has specific functionality for Nordic countries within Convergent Invoicing:

  • Outgoing CI invoices automatically generate and display reference numbers to comply with government regulations [3]
  • Payment reference logic varies based on payment format:
    • SEPA Direct Debit: Uses Official Document Number (ODN) if available, otherwise invoice number
    • Direct Debit with mandate: Uses ODN if available, otherwise invoice number
    • Direct Debit without mandate: Uses FIK number [3]
Option 3: Document Information Extraction Enhancement

Recent SAP enhancements include payment reference extraction capabilities:

  • Document Information Extraction service (OCR) now extracts Payment Reference fields from invoices [4]
  • This feature is automatically enabled and supports Nordic countries' requirements
  • Useful for inbound invoice processing but may not directly address outgoing invoice generation
Option 4: Custom Reference Fields Integration

For payment processing, you can include custom reference fields in payment medium formats:

  • Map custom fields to payment format structures [5]
  • Implement custom logic for payment reference handling
  • This approach focuses on payment file generation rather than invoice creation

Implementation Steps

  1. Requirement Validation: Confirm whether Contract Accounting or Convergent Invoicing modules are available in your template
  2. Module Assessment: Evaluate if your invoice processes can leverage Contract Accounting functionality
  3. Configuration Setup: If applicable, configure payment reference models and activate generation for your Norwegian company code
  4. Testing Phase: Validate payment reference generation in test invoices and verify bank statement reconciliation
  5. Template Rollout: Deploy configuration across Norwegian entities and extend to Sweden/Finland as needed

Technical Considerations

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud restricts direct field substitution for BSEG-KIDNO to maintain financial posting consistency [1]
  • Contract Accounting module provides the most viable standard solution for payment reference auto-generation [2]
  • Convergent Invoicing offers country-specific functionality but may require module licensing [3]
  • Any custom development would require feature requests through SAP Customer Influence Portal [1]

Risks and Mitigation

  • Module Dependency: Solution effectiveness depends on available SAP modules in your license
  • Template Compatibility: Ensure chosen approach aligns with your global template design
  • Upgrade Impact: Standard SAP functionality ensures future upgrade compatibility
  • Fallback Option: Manual payment reference entry may be required if standard solutions are unavailable

Next Steps

  1. Immediate Assessment: Verify if Contract Accounting or Convergent Invoicing modules are included in your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud license
  2. Stakeholder Alignment: Discuss module-based solutions with your project team and business stakeholders
  3. Feature Request: If standard solutions are insufficient, submit enhancement requests via SAP Customer Influence Portal [1]
  4. Nordic Rollout Planning: Coordinate implementation approach across Norway, Sweden, and Finland requirements

Would you like me to help you assess which SAP modules are available in your current license, or would you prefer guidance on configuring the Contract Accounting payment reference functionality?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration – Cloud based ERP Synchronization of Technical Objects
entry.content: Good question @JOSE-ODAR. BNAC itself is not a mobility application and does not require a separate mobile link. However, the integration between BNAC and ERP currently relies on the MAIF (Mobile Add-on Integration Framework) components available in the ERP backend.

entry.title: Re: SAC Calendar time to complete actions in Parallel
entry.content:
I am not aware of any general systemic reason why running two multi-actions or data actions in parallel should take significantly more time then running the same sequentially.

Generally the only constraints would be the number of virtual CPU and memory of your tenant, so unless you are at near capacity due to high activity this should not have significant impact.

There could be a bandwith issue if you are running parallel data actions that read from or write to an external system (live data models or writing to S/4HANA for instance, but we would need to analyze your system and process to determine if any of the above are the case. For this issue I would suggest you open a support incident with SAP so that we can investigate this further.

entry.title: How do Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant work together in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently preparing for my Sourcing and Procurement  certification and studying the system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. I have a question regarding the two workspaces in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) and how they relate to the overall landscape.

In CBC, when selecting the Deployment Target, I can see two available targets:
- SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape — Training Customizing (Entitlement Role: Training Customizing)
- SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape — Training Development (Entitlement Role: Training Development)

 

From what I understand:
- One CBC workspace is connected to the Customizing Tenant
- A second CBC workspace is connected to the Development Tenant
- Both are part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud development system

My background: I have worked on SAP Private Cloud / On-Premise projects where we had a classic 4-system landscape (D, Q, R, P) with customizing and development handled together in the D system and transported in one direction through the landscape.

My questions are:

1. Do the Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant each have their own independent transport landscape (e.g. each with their own Q, R and P systems)? Or do they share the same downstream systems?

2. How do developments or extensions built in the Development Tenant eventually reach the production environment? Is there a transport mechanism between the two tenants at any point?

3. Where do business users perform UAT — only in the Customizing Tenant, or also in the Development Tenant? If both tenants need to be tested, does this mean UAT must be conducted twice?

Thank you very much for any clarification!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Clarification on data monitor tasks when running cons...
entry.content:
Hello Manas,

Well the answer is simple: the consolidation for the last period of the year is the consolidation for the year. The basis for consolidation is YTD data (ACDOCU stores YTD data). If they don't want monthly or periodic consolidation, the global parameters for the last period and fiscal year will give the yearly consolidation. We don't need to run the data monitor or consolidation tasks for the last but one periods at all. In the reports please select YTD instead of Periodic to see the correct result for the full year consolidation.  

Regards.

entry.title: 財務リーダーが価格戦略で「ゲームを変える」時代 ―― SAP CPQが切り拓く企業価値向上の可能性
entry.content:
はじめに：価格設定はなぜ経営課題なのか

B2B企業において、価格の設定方法は企業価値（株価）に対して深い影響を及ぼします。それは単に「製品にいくら付けるか」という問題ではなく、組織全体の価格計算に用いるメソドロジー（方法論）そのものが問題の本質です。

価格設定メソドロジーは、営業・マーケティング・財務の各部門をつなぐ共通言語として組織に深く根付いています。だからこそ、それが「制度化」され、「ずっとこのやり方でやってきた」という固定観念として、競争優位のための変革が妨げられるケースが後を絶ちません。

本稿では、価格設定メソドロジーがいかに企業価値に影響を与え得るか、そしてそれをどのように刷新することで大きな価値創出が可能かを、具体的な事例とともに解説します。財務リーダーの方々には特に、この「見落とされがちな競争優位の源泉」に注目していただきたいと思います。

価格の種類とメソドロジー：「価格」という言葉の多様性を理解する

「価格」という言葉は多様な意味を持ちます。B2B取引においては、以下のような種類が存在します。

  • 定価（List Price）：企業が公表する標準的な販売価格。ディスカウントの起点として機能します。
  • 実勢価格（Street Price）：顧客が実際に支払う平均的な価格。定価からの典型的な割引額を差し引いた水準です。
  • フロア価格（Floor Price）：営業担当者が上位承認なしに提示できる最低価格。
  • 取引価格（Transaction Price）：特定の顧客が特定の取引で実際に支払った価格。ERPや財務システムに記録されます。
  • 価格リスト（Price Lists）：業種別・顧客別など複数の価格体系。システムは優先順位に従い最適な価格を自動判定する必要があります。

価格設定の方法論としても、コストプラス価格法・競合価格法・バリューベース価格法・ダイナミックプライシング・マージンベース価格法・地域/チャネル別価格法など多岐にわたります。一つの見積書の中で複数のメソドロジーが混在するケースも少なくありません。

重要な点は、ほとんどのB2B企業は長年同じ価格メソドロジーを使い続けており、変更できると考えてすらいないという現実です。しかし、価格メソドロジーの刷新こそが、競争優位を生む大きな機会となり得ます。

価格設定を「営業ツール」として活用する

調査によると、正確で完全な見積書を最初に提示した売り手が、取引のほとんどを勝ち取ることが示されています。これは「アンカリング効果」と呼ばれる認知バイアスに基づくもので、意思決定において最初に提示された情報が強い影響力を持つためです。

また、顧客はより簡単で快適な購買体験を求めています。かつてBlockbusterでビデオをレンタルする際に延滞料のストレスを感じていたように、複雑な価格体系や煩雑な見積プロセスはB2B顧客を遠ざけます。Netflixが延滞料なしのサービスでゲームを変えたように、シンプルで分かりやすい価格体系が受注率の向上に直結します。

さらに、価格設定は成約タイミングのコントロールにも活用できます。期限付き割引による早期クロージングの誘導、あるいは将来購買へのインセンティブ付与による収益の平準化など、営業戦略と価格設定は密接に連動します。

効率的な見積プロセスは、営業担当者が同期間内により多くの見積を作成することを可能にし、販売コストの削減につながります。 B2B企業では通常、見積の約半数から三分の一しか受注に結び付かないため、見積作成の効率化は収益性改善において非常に重要です。

価格設定を「マーケティングツール」として活用する

価格は、Webサイトや広告などマーケティング活動における価値訴求の手段にもなります。「5部屋一律〇〇円」のような分かりやすい価格設定は、プロモーションで活用しやすく、受注率向上に寄与します。

また、Progressive Insuranceが販売チャネルによって価格を変えているように、価格設計によって顧客を特定のチャネルに誘導することも可能です。逆にAppleは全チャネルで統一価格を維持し、顧客に「必ず最良の価格で購入できる」という安心感を与えています。

選択肢の数も重要な要素です。 選択肢が少なすぎると顧客は制限を感じ、多すぎると「選択オーバーロード」が発生します。自動車メーカーが「トリムレベル（グレード別パッケージ）」を設定することで顧客の選択を簡略化しているように、B2B見積においても提示するオプション数を適切にコントロールすることが購買意思決定を促進します。

価格設定を「財務ツール」として活用する ―― CFOへのメッセージ

財務部門がとりわけ注目すべき点は、価格設定が企業の収益性・財務リスク管理・企業価値に直接影響することです。

1. 利益率に基づくディスカウント管理
CPQ（Configure, Price, Quote）ソフトウェアは、製品コストを営業担当者に開示することなく、見積の利益率の健全性を「赤・黄・緑」のインジケーターで可視化できます。これにより、機密情報を守りながら、全社的に利益率を意識した値引き管理が実現します。

2. 為替リスクの軽減
海外市場に販売する企業は為替変動リスクに直面します。CPQソフトウェアは、入金予定日に基づいて見積価格と通貨を動的に調整し、為替ヘッジ戦略と連携した価格管理を実現します。

3. 製品設定ミスによる損失（Write-down）の防止
製造不可能な構成で誤って見積もりを提示してしまった場合、追加コストや値引きで対応せざるを得ず、その分が直接利益を圧迫します。CPQの製品コンフィギュレーター機能により、製造可能な組み合わせのみが見積可能となり、こうした損失を事前に防止できます。

4. M&Aシナジーの早期実現
企業買収後、統合製品ラインのバンドル構成と価格設定を迅速に行うことで、投資家に約束した「買収の効果実現スピード」を高めることができます。CPQはこのプロセスを大幅に加速します。

5. 規制対応と正確な売上予測
販売可能な地域・対象顧客の自動チェックや見積書への必要記載事項の自動挿入により、コンプライアンスリスクを低減します。また、見積更新のたびにCRMの商談情報が自動更新されることで、財務アナリストが重視する売上予測の精度向上にも貢献します。上場企業にとって、予測精度は株式評価にも影響を与える重要な指標です。

まとめ：CPQは「価格革新」を実現するインフラ

Blockbusterはその商品価格でNetflixに負けたわけではありません。価格のメソドロジーと体験設計で敗れたのです。同様に、B2B企業においても、製品力だけでなく価格戦略の高度化が競争優位を生む時代が来ています。

多くの企業がより洗練された価格戦略を採用しない理由は、「メールとスプレッドシートでは実装できないから」です。SAP CPQはまさにそのギャップを埋めるソリューションとして設計されており、コストプラスからバリューベース、ダイナミックプライシングに至る多様な価格メソドロジーを実装可能にします。

財務リーダーにとって、価格設定メソドロジーの刷新は、大きな企業価値向上の機会です。 今こそ、「ずっとこのやり方でやってきた」という固定観念を見直し、CPQを活用した価格戦略の変革を検討してみてください。

著者：Bill Butler（SAP CPQ グローバルプロダクトマーケティングマネージャー）

原文はこちらでご確認ください。
SAP CPQソリューションの詳細は、SAPの公式サイトをご覧ください。

entry.title: BSP component view rendering issue while launching from the fiori launchpad
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S4 Hana system. 

I have created one custom CRM BSP component (T Code : BSP_WD_CMPWB) with form view having multiple buttons for data processing from excel sheet uploaded from the desktop.

We have created Fiori tile to launch this custom BSP component. 

But What I have observed that as soon as I create event handler for two of the buttons created for data validation and data processing, all buttons get disappeared. 

It would be really helpful if member could share their views for this strange behavior.

Thanks

Vinod Yadav

entry.title: 5W2 Group Evaluation of Initial Go-Live Switch
entry.content: We are implementing the SCOPE ITEM 5W2 group cost assessment function this time. For the group assessment, what initial preparations are needed for the go-live transition? For example, data collection, data import, etc.
